cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the story again and see what the question is asking. The question is about why the employer made all those criticisms about Julia Vassilyevna's work. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to understand the story. The employer is calculating how much he owes Julia, his governess. He starts by saying they agreed on thirty rubles a month, but Julia says forty. He insists on thirty. He then subtracts days she didn't work, like Sundays, holidays, days the kids were sick, etc. He keeps deducting money from her pay, bringing it down from 60 to 41, then subtracts more for broken items and other incidents. Finally, he gives her 11 rubles instead of the 80 she actually deserves. But then he gets angry when she says "merci" because he feels guilty for cheating her.
